$schema: engine backlog (compiled from gks/atoms.gorch.json — do NOT hand-edit; run `node gks/compile.mjs`)

tasks.title: Genesis Atom Schema — Define the GenesisAtom type: immutable slug `id` + mutable `displayName`, type, hierarchy (masterplan..subtask), context_scaling_tier H0-H6, status, body, first-class rice/moscow/requiresConfirm, and the ownership block (owner/borrow/lease/fence)
tasks.accept: TS/JSON-schema for GenesisAtom committed; round-trips Markdown<->object; passes GKS-001/002 on a sample set.

tasks.title: Genesis Compile (decompose runtime) — The first runtime primitive (seeded by gks/compile.mjs): read atoms -> validate GKS-001 (unique id) / GKS-002 (acyclic) / GKS-003 (>6 hops warn) -> assemble a runnable engine backlog + render canonical Markdown
tasks.accept: `node gks/compile.mjs` validates + emits backlog.gorch.json + atom .md; fails loudly on dup id / cycle / unresolved dep.

tasks.title: Doc-to-Code Pipeline — The closed loop: intent (Copilot/Spec) -> author atoms -> compile to backlog -> agent claims -> build scoped by H-tier -> Verify Gate -> record outcome+trace back to the graph
tasks.accept: Runbook doc lists every step with the owning module; a worked example traces one atom end-to-end.

tasks.title: Tauri v2 + React Shell — Scaffold the Tauri v2 + Rust shell with a React/Vite webview, wrapping the proven engine.mjs as a supervised sidecar (strangler-around-a-sidecar)
tasks.accept: `pnpm tauri dev` opens the desktop window; webview renders a placeholder Board fed by engine snapshot over IPC.

tasks.title: Engine IPC Bridge — Tauri commands + events + channels bridging the Rust shell and the engine sidecar: snapshot/state pull, command dispatch (claim/done/dispatch/run/stop), and a live log stream channel for the Cockpit.
tasks.accept: UI receives live snapshot + log stream; a dispatch command round-trips to the engine and mutates state.

tasks.title: GenesisDB Node Sidecar — Greenfield: a thin Node sidecar hosting the GenesisDB N-API binary in-process (no port, dodging :3000)
tasks.accept: Sidecar loads the pinned binary, addNode + hybridSearch + retrieveContext round-trip with bge-m3 vectors; refuses on schemaVersion mismatch.

tasks.title: Knowledge Adapter (file | genesisdb) — A single swappable adapter (recordOutcome/queryContext/asOf/linkTrace) with a MANDATORY flat-file fallback
tasks.accept: Same API passes in BOTH file and genesisdb modes; off-Windows auto-degrades to file; semantic query falls back to lexical when embeddings are down.

tasks.title: Cost-Cap (tier-bound) — Replace usageLimits=null with non-null, license-tier-bound session/weekly USD caps (Free low -> Pro/Studio higher), user-editable within the tier ceiling, + a global kill-switch
tasks.accept: Pool auto-stops at the configured session/weekly cap; cold-start uses the tier seed; kill-switch halts dispatch immediately.

tasks.title: Governance Gate — Human-confirm gate triggered by an explicit `requiresConfirm` field PLUS auto for Safety::*/deploy/merge/irreversible
tasks.accept: A gated atom cannot transition claimed->running without an explicit human confirm; non-gated atoms flow normally.

tasks.title: AtomStore (one object, six lenses) — A single normalized client store (keyed by atom id) so Board card, Graph node, Canvas node, Copilot output, and Cockpit tile are all views of the same row — no parallel models
tasks.accept: Mutating one atom updates every surface from one store; no duplicated task/graph models.

tasks.title: Board (Jira+Trello+Linear) — Status/phase columns, claimable cards, RICE/MoSCoW (first-class atom fields), and a rich task-card modal (FR/version/DOD/changelog/export)
tasks.accept: Drag a card to claim/assign; columns reflect state with no empty-column clutter; card modal shows + edits atom fields.

tasks.title: Agent Loadout (inventory) — The game-inventory surface: per role equip Model (hat), Tools/Capabilities, Skills, MemoryOS, context scope+tier, persona+authority
tasks.accept: Drag gear onto slots; stats (cost/speed/capability) render; the equipped loadout actually routes the next dispatch.

tasks.title: Copilot Console — Chat-to-command surface with a Maiden-style persona: natural language -> author atoms / dispatch waves via slash-commands, shown as atom diffs
tasks.accept: A natural-language ask produces atoms or a dispatch; banner appears when Ollama embeddings are down.

tasks.title: Live Cockpit — Real-time agent tiles (claimed/running/reviewing), streaming logs via the IPC channel, a cost gauge reading the tier-bound caps, and intervene controls (pause/stop/kill-switch).
tasks.accept: Running agents and live cost render in real time; the kill-switch and pause act immediately.

tasks.title: Planner Tiering — The Planner owns the H-tier heuristic: assign the lowest tier that suffices by WBS rung (subtask->H0 .
tasks.accept: Atoms get a sane default tier by rung; near-cap runs downgrade tier; an H0 task literally cannot glob.

tasks.title: Verify Gate v2 — PASS if no `critical` issues (issue-count/severity, not a trusted score)
tasks.accept: Non-critical output auto-advances; offline/cap routes review to the local judge; governance tasks still require human confirm.

tasks.title: Ownership Layer (agent borrow-checker) — Model claim/lease as Rust ownership: claim = exclusive &mut (<=1), context read = shared & (unlimited), lease = lifetime, move = fencing token
tasks.accept: Implement as a STANDALONE, unit-testable module orchestration/gks/ownership.mjs (in-memory borrow store: acquire/release/move; <=1 exclusive holder; unlimited shared readers; lease expiry; fencing token rejects stale holders). Do NOT modify engine.mjs or any existing file — create only the new module (+ optional gks/ownership.test.mjs). Acceptance: two agents cannot exclusive-borrow the same atom (conflict rejected); readers never block the writer; stale-fence holders are rejected.

tasks.title: Gold-set Eval Harness — A labeled gold-set + scorer to validate planner tiering + Verify-Gate decisions BEFORE auto-spend is trusted
tasks.accept: Harness scores tier-assignment + review verdicts vs labels; auto-spend stays disabled until a threshold is met.

tasks.title: In-app Updater — Mirror G-Maiden's release machinery: Tauri updater + minisign-signed installers + latest.json feed + deferred install
tasks.accept: A signed update downloads, drains active tasks, and applies on next launch; tampered signatures are rejected.

tasks.title: License & Entitlement — Per-machine device-bound entitlement (ed25519/PASETO v4.local) + offline grace window
tasks.accept: A device-bound token unlocks the tier offline within grace; tampering/expiry degrades to Free; updater stays free across tiers.

tasks.title: Telemetry (opt-in) — Opt-in, OFF by default (honors local-first)
tasks.accept: Nothing leaves the machine unless opted in; the payload is restricted to the counter allowlist.

tasks.title: Editable Graph (Obsidian) — View AND edit the dependency/relation graph: nodes=atoms, edges=wikilinks; create/edit/delete edges by dragging; traverse, filter, focus
tasks.accept: Dragging creates/removes a real dependency edge (acyclic-checked, GKS-002); focus depth maps to H-tier.

tasks.title: Node<->DB Canvas — Visual canvas to drop nodes/files/atoms INTO GenesisBlockDB and render only selected atoms/files back FROM the DB
tasks.accept: Dropping a node persists it to GenesisDB; a query renders only the selected atoms back onto the canvas.

tasks.title: MemoryOS (per-agent KB) — A long-term, persistent, private per-agent knowledge base backed by GenesisBlockDB (graph+vector) OR filesystem
tasks.accept: An agent writes + recalls private memory across sessions; backend swaps file<->genesisdb without API change.

tasks.title: Traceability Graph (bitemporal) — Promote the anti-error loop from a failure-only side-channel into the primary atom-keyed traceability backbone: edges implemented_by/verified_by/depends_on/supersedes/failed_with + bitemporal asOf/validFrom/causedBy.
tasks.accept: Given an atom, the graph answers which spec asked, which task built, which test proved, and what was believed when shipped.

tasks.title: Multi-host Pool — Replace the single-host .state.lock with DB-backed fencing-token leased claims owned by a Coordinator host (the ownership/fence schema designed in P1 makes this incremental)
tasks.accept: Two hosts claim from one DB without double-execution; a fenced stale claim is rejected; Coordinator failover defined.

tasks.title: Cross-platform Build — napi cross-compile CI matrix (linux-gnu / win-msvc / darwin x64+arm64) + signed release assets + npm/binary publish, lifting the Windows-first limit so mac/Linux get semantic recall (not just flat-file).
tasks.accept: CI produces signed binaries for all targets; mac/Linux load GenesisDB N-API and pass the knowledge-adapter suite.

tasks.title: GoVibe MCP Bridge (SUPERSEDED -> A2A surface) — [SUPERSEDED by protocol--a2a-surface -- ADR-O-006: generalized to an open, consumer-agnostic A2A interop surface; GoVibe is reference-only
tasks.accept: A versioned boundary contract doc exists; v1 atom/event schema is verified compatible with it.

tasks.title: Marketplace Seam — Design the seam (not the storefront) for sharing/selling agent loadouts/templates/skins (rarity tiers, like the Maiden Atelier)
tasks.accept: A signed loadout pack imports + verifies; the seam is documented so a later storefront drops in without schema change.

tasks.title: A2A Interop Surface (contract) — Open Agent-to-Agent (A2A) interop seam; supersedes protocol--govibe-mcp-bridge (ADR-O-006)
tasks.accept: A versioned A2A contract exists (Agent Card schema + Task<->8-state mapping + the atoms/events crossing the seam + A2A-remote-as-Provider registration); end-to-end, an external A2A agent can delegate a task to G-Orch and receive an artifact, and G-Orch can delegate one task out via the registry, both passing the pre-dispatch governance gate.

tasks.title: AutoLoop (autonomous build-test-benchmark-refine) — The autonomous self-improvement engine ('Autonomas')
tasks.accept: Given a goal atom + gold-set, the loop runs unattended and either hits the benchmark target or stops on a defined termination condition (never runs away on cost); every round is recorded in the traceability graph with its score; cost-cap and gates are enforced on every BUILD round; a mid-loop crash resumes from the last checkpoint.
